content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a literal "TODO" placeholder (App/Info.plist), and NSCameraUsageDescription is generic boilerplate ("This app needs camera access.") not tied to the actual scanning feature seen in ScannerView.swift.
Pierre: Apple reviewers reject apps with placeholder or generic purpose strings because they don't inform users why the permission is needed, and "TODO" is an obvious sign of unfinished work. Also, there's no visible feature in the app that uses location at all, so this permission may be unused/unjustified. Fix the camera string to describe the document-scanning use case (e.g. "SnapScan uses your camera to scan documents") and either remove the location permission if unused or write a specific description tied to a real feature.
